questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If f : $R \rightarrow R, f(x)$ is a differentiable function such that $(f(x))^2=c^2+\int_0^x\left(f(t)^2+\left(f^{\prime}(t)\right)^2\right) d t \quad \forall x \in R$. Possible value(s) of $f(1)$ is/are
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 2 f(x) f(x)=(f(x))^2+(f(x))^2 \\ & \Rightarrow\left[f(x)-f^{\prime}(x)\right]^2=0 \Rightarrow f(x)=f(x) \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)= \pm e^{(x+1)} \\ & \Rightarrow f(1)= \pm e^2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a function $y=f(x)$ passes through the point $\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{\ln 2}}, \frac{1}{2}\right)$ and satisfies the differential equation $x^2 d y-2 e^{\frac{-1}{x^2}} d x=0$, then : (Assume $\left.f(0)=0\right)$
options: $y=f(x)$ has exactly one point of inflection.
$y=f(x)$ has exactly two points of inflection.
questions.solution: $f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{2 e^{-1 / x^2}}{x^2} < \frac{2 e^{-1 / x^2}}{x^3} \forall x \in(0,1 / \sqrt{2})$ $f(x) < e^{-1 / x^2}$ Let $g(x)=e^{-1 / x^2}$ $\int_0^{1 / \sqrt{2}} f(x) d x < \int_0^{1 / \sqrt{2}} g(x) d x$ Now, $\quad g^{\prime}(x)=\frac{2 e^{-1 / x^2}}{x^3}$ $g^{\prime \prime}(x)=e^{-1 / x^2}\left(\frac{4}{x^6}-\frac{6}{x^4}\right)>0$ $\Rightarrow \quad \frac{-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}} < x < \frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}$ $\therefore \quad y=g(x)$ is concave up $\forall x \in(0,1 / \sqrt{2})$ $\Rightarrow \quad \int_0^{1 / \sqrt{2}} g(x) d x < \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2} e^2} \Rightarrow \int_0^{1 / \sqrt{2}} f(x) d x < \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2} e^2} \quad \Rightarrow$(a) For point of inflection $f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{2 e^{-1 / x^2}}{x^2}$ Now, $f^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ gives quadratic in $x$, hence 2 point of inflection $\Rightarrow(d)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow R$ be a differentiable function satisfying the equation $f(x y)=e^{x y-x-y}\left(e^y f(x)+e^x f(y)\right) \forall x, y>0$. If $f^{\prime}(1)=e$, then:
options: number of roots of the equation $f(x)=x e^x$ in $(0, \infty)$ is 2 .
$f(x)$ is a strictly increasing function in $(0, \infty)$.
questions.solution:
$f(x y)=e^{x y-x} \cdot f(x)+e^{x y-y} f(y)$
Differentiating w.r.t. $x$
$y f^{\prime}(x y)=e^{x y-x} f^{\prime}(x)+f(x) e^{x y-x}(y-1)+f(y) e^{x y-y} \cdot y$
Put $x=1$
$y f^{\prime}(y)=e^{y-1} f^{\prime}(1)+f(1) e^{y-1}(y-1)+y f(y)$
Now put $x=y=1 ; \quad f(1)=0$
$\therefore \quad y f^{\prime}(y)=e^y+y f(y) \quad \Rightarrow \quad x\left(f^{\prime}(x)-f(x)\right)=e^x$
$e^{-x}\left(f^{\prime}(x)-f(x)\right)=\frac{1}{x}$
Integrate both sides
$e^{-x} f(x)=\ln x+C$
$f(1)=0 \quad \therefore \quad C=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad f(x)=e^x \ln x$. Now verify.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The differential equation of the curve for which intercept cut by any tangent on $y$-axis is equal to the length of the sub normal -
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$ be the curve.
Equation of tangent at $(x, y)$ will be
$\mathrm{Y}-\mathrm{y}=f^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})(\mathrm{X}-\mathrm{x})$
Intercept cut by the tangent on $y$-axis will be $y-x f^{\prime}(x)$
so $y-x f^{\prime}(x)=y \frac{d y}{d x} \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x+y}$
This is homogeneous equation of first degree \& first order.
Rewriting this equation $\frac{d x}{d y}-\frac{x}{y}=1$ which is a linear equation.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $y$ satisfies the differential equation $x d y=y\left(x y^3+1\right) d x \quad \& y(1)=2$, then -
questions.solution:
$x \frac{d y}{d x}-y=x y^4$
Dividing both sides by $\mathrm{y}^4 \&$ putting $\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}^3}=\mathrm{t}$
$\Rightarrow \quad-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{3} \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}-\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{x} \quad$ or $\quad \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\frac{3 \mathrm{t}}{\mathrm{x}}=-3$
which on solving gives $\frac{x^3}{y^3}=\frac{-3 x^4}{4}+c$
$\because \quad y(1)=2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad c=\frac{7}{8}$
Hence $\mathrm{y}^3=\frac{8 \mathrm{x}^3}{7-6 \mathrm{x}^4} \quad \Rightarrow \quad f(\mathrm{x})=\sqrt[3]{\frac{8 \mathrm{x}^3}{7-6 \mathrm{x}^4}}$
which is odd and discontinuous at point where $7-6 x^4=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If differential equation corresponding to family of curves $y=A \cos 2 x+B \sin ^2 x+C$ is given by $\lambda \frac{\mathrm{d}^3 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}^3}+f(\mathrm{x}) \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}^2}=2 \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\cos 2 \mathrm{x})$, where $\lambda$ is real constant and $f(\mathrm{x})$ is some function in x , then -
options: Number of solution of equation $f(x)=\lambda$ in $(0,4)$ are 2
Number of solution of equation $f(x)=\lambda$ in $(0,4)$ are 3
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & y=A \cos 2 x+B \sin ^2 x+C \\ & y=A\left(1-2 \sin ^2 x\right)+B \sin ^2 x+C \\ & \Rightarrow y=k_1+k_2 \sin ^2 x \quad \Rightarrow y^{\prime}=k_2 \sin 2 x \\ & \Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}=k_2 2 \cos 2 x \quad \Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}=\frac{2 y^{\prime} \cos 2 x}{\sin 2 x} \\ & \Rightarrow \sin 2 x \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=2 \frac{d y}{d x}(\cos 2 x)\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \lambda=0 \& f(x)=\sin 2 x$
Solution of $\sin 2 x=0$ in $(0,4)$ are $x=\frac{\pi}{2} \& \pi$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The solution of $\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)\left(x^2 y^3+x y\right)=1$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d y}=x^2 y^3+x y \\ & \frac{d x}{d y}-x y=x^2 y^3 \\ & -\frac{1}{x^2} \frac{d x}{d y}+\frac{y}{x}=-y^3 \\ & \text { Put } \frac{1}{x}=t\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a continuous function $f(x)$ satisfy the relation $\int_0^x t f(x-t) d t=\int_0^x t f(t) d t+\sin x+\cos x-x-1$, for all real number $x$, then which of the following does not hold good
questions.solution:
Given equation can be written as 
$\int_0^x x f(t) d t-\int_0^x t f(t) d t=\int_0^x f(t) d t+\sin x+\cos x-x-1$
differentiating
$x f(x)+\int_0^x f(t) d t-x f(x)=f(x)+\cos x-\sin x-1$ again differentiating
$f(x)=f^{\prime}(x)-\sin x-\cos x$ which is linear differential equation of $1^{\text {st }}$ order whose solution gives
$\begin{aligned} & y e^{-x}=-e^{-x} \cos x+c \\ & x=0, y=0 \Rightarrow c=1 \\ & f(x)=e^x-\cos x\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A function $\mathrm{f}: \mathrm{R} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}$ satisfies $\sin 2 x \sin 2 y\{f(3 x+3 y)+f(3 x-3 y)\}=\cos 2 x \cos 2 y\{f(3 x-3 y)-f(3 x+3 y) ;$ if $f(0)=1$ then
questions.solution:
$\sin 2 x \sin 2 y\{f(3 x+3 y)+f(3 x-3 y)\}=\cos 2 x \cos 2 y\{f(3 x-3 y)-f(3 x+3 y)\}$
$(f 3 x+3 y) \cos (2 x-2 y)=f(3 x-3 y) \cos (2 x+2 y)$
$\frac{f(3 x+3 y)}{f(3 x-3 y)}=\frac{\cos (2 x+2 y)}{\cos (2 x-2 y)} \Rightarrow \frac{f(3 x+3 y)}{\cos (2 x+2 y)}=\frac{f(3 x-3 y)}{\cos (2 x-2 y)}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{f(\alpha)}{\cos \left(\frac{2 \alpha}{3}\right)}=\frac{f(\beta)}{\cos \left(\frac{2 \beta}{3}\right)}=k \Rightarrow f(x)=k \cos \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\cos \left(\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{3}\right)$ $\qquad$ (1) $\{\because f(0)=1]$
$f^{\prime}(x)=-\frac{2}{3} \sin \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)$    .....(ii)
$f^{\prime \prime}(x)=-\frac{4}{9} \cos \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)$    ....(iii)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $y=P(x)$ be a differentiable function $\forall x \in[0, \infty)$ such that $\frac{d}{d x}(P(x))+(x-1)^3 \geq P(x)+1 \forall x \in[0, \infty)$. If $P(x) \leq x^3+3 x+1 \forall x \in[0, \infty)$ and $P(0)=1$ then which of the following is/are correct?
options: Area bounded by $y=P(x) ; x$-axis; $x=0$ and $x=1$ is $\frac{11}{4}$
questions.solution:
$\frac{d}{d x}(P(x))+(x-1)^3-(P(x)+1) \geq 0$
$\Rightarrow \quad e^{-x}\left(\frac{d P(x)}{d x}-P(x)+x^3-3 x^2+3 x-2\right) \geq 0$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d}{d x}\left(P(x) e^{-x}\right)-\frac{d}{d x} e^{-x} x^3-3 \frac{d}{d x} x e^{-x}-\frac{d}{d x} e^{-x} \geq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d}{d x}\left(P(x)-\left(x^3+3 x+1\right)\right) e^{-x} \geq 0\end{aligned}$
Let $g(x)=\left(P(x)-\left(x^3+3 x+1\right)\right) e^{-x}$ is increasing
$g(x) \geq g(0) \Rightarrow\left(P(x)-\left(x^3+3 x+1\right)\right) e^{-x} \geq 0 \forall x \geq 0$
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { but } & P(x) \leq x^3+3 x+1 \forall x \geq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & P(x)=x^3+3 x+1 \forall x \geq 0\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
